publicationstmt.publisher: Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections

profiledesc.creation: This finding aid was produced using ArchivesSpace on 2023-10-03 00:18:52 UTC.
profiledesc.langusage: Description is written in: Undetermined, Code for undetermined script script.

repository.corpname: Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections

scopecontent.head: Content Description
scopecontent.p: Collection contains Buhrman's teaching and lecture materials, correspondence, conference and research files, and department files. Includes materials pertaining to Buhrman's research on nanoscale magnets and superconducting devices.

bioghist.head: Biographical / Historical
bioghist.p: Robert "Bob" Buhrman, Cornell University M.S. '69, Ph.D. '73, was the John Edson Sweet Memorial Professor of Engineering Emeritus and Cornell's Second Senior Vice Provost for Research, and Vice President for Technology Transfer and Intellectual Property. He helped expand emerging science and engineering programs, and obtain funding for research. Buhrman was the Founding Director of the Cornell Center for Nanoscale Systems in Information Technologies (CNS). His areas of research interest included applied condensed matter physics with emphasis on nanoscale magnetism and spintronics.

userestrict.head: Conditions Governing Use
userestrict.p: Corresponce restricted to permission of curator.

controlaccess.subject: Engineering.
controlaccess.geogname: Cornell University. School of Applied and Engineering Physics
controlaccess.subject: Cornell University--Alumni and alumnae

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Professional correspondence dating back to Buhrman's tenure as Assistant Professor in 1973.

processinfo.head: Processing Information
processinfo.p: Correspondence originally kept chronologically in binders.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Course materials include lecture notes and transparencies, exams, lab manuals, and syllabi.

did.unittitle: Applied physics -- qualifying exams -- 96 qualifier material
RAB -- Quantum mechanics -- an exam draft

Statistical mechanics qualifying exam question. Qual. Exam 1982

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Arranged by course number as original folders and binders were primarily labelled with course number only.

processinfo.head: Processing Information
processinfo.p: Course lists containing grades and student social security numbers removed.

processinfo.head: Processing Information
processinfo.p: Materials originally filed separately from Buhrman's personal course materials.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Buhrman's files on Applied and Engineering Physics (AEP) department and the College of Engineering. Includes records on the Cornell Materials Science Center and the Cornell Nanofabrication Facility.

processinfo.head: Processing Information
processinfo.p: Files originally kept in hanging folders. Relevant digital media interfiled by archivist.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Also contains photographs of Buhrman with students in Clark Hall.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains papers presented at conferences, and materials for lectures and talks. Series contains many transparencies.

processinfo.head: Processing Information
processinfo.p: Related digital media interfiled by archivist.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Reports and bound committee meeting materials for entities that Buhrman was involved in, both Cornell and non-Cornell affiliated, which include the College of Engineering, the Cornell Center for Materials Research, and Harvard's Center for Nanoscale Systems in Information Technologies.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Files represent Buhrman's tenure at Cornell both as a student and faculty member. Records include his PhD program acceptance letter, photographs, personal faculty business records, and correspondence.

did.unittitle: Academic and TA stuff and JHU transcript
Personal file. NAS/NAE (National Academy of Engineering) nomination
Personal file. Director appointment '88 - salary letters
